failure_reason: Two proposed relations. The corridor relation is supported by block p004_c0004 mentioning 'the corridor' and metric scope '34th Street corridor'. But the project relation fails: the cited block text never mentions 'Transitway' or any project name — only corridor facts. Co-occurrence in a Transitway CAC presentation slide is not a relation per rules.

Project relation unsupported by cited block text. Block p004_c0004 lists corridor facts ('90% of travelers on the corridor') but never mentions the Transitway project. Co-occurrence in project slides is not a relation.

Project relation unsupported: block p004_c0004 states '33,000+ daily bus riders' with no mention of Transitway or the project. The corridor relation is fine but the project relation fails the co-occurrence rule.
Project relation unsupported by cited block. 'Over 80% of these residents do not own a car' describes corridor-proximate residents; block never mentions Transitway. Co-occurrence ≠ relation.

Project relation unsupported: block p004_c0004 gives 'Over 80% of these workers commute by transit or walking' — corridor demographics, not a statement about the Transitway project. Co-occurrence rule violated.
Project relation unsupported: 'About 50,000 residents and 300,000 workers within ¼ mile' is a corridor buffer statistic. Block never mentions Transitway. Co-occurrence is not a relation.
Project relation unsupported: same block, same issue — worker count is a corridor statistic. No Transitway mention in cited text.

Cited block p009_c0002 says 'Curb access is key to businesses and the quality of life of residents' — a survey finding with no mention of the Transitway project. Co-occurrence in project slides is insufficient for has_claim relation.
Cited block p009_c0002 gives 'Highest frequency of curb side activities is during daytime hours' — no Transitway mention. Pure co-occurrence.
Cited block p009_c0002: 'Curb side needs vary by building type and by side street access' — a survey finding with no Transitway mention. Co-occurrence is not a relation.

Cited bullet from p010_c0002 is 'Certain curb activities must occur right in front of buildings, e.g. Access-A-Ride, oil deliveries' — this bullet does not mention Transitway. The third bullet in same block does mention Transitway, but the cited bullet does not. Co-occurrence across bullets in same block is not sufficient.
